speaker: LEONATO
text: Come, Friar Francis, be brief, only to the

speaker: LEONATO
text: plain form of marriage, and you shall recount their

speaker: LEONATO
text: particular duties afterwards.

speaker: FRIAR
text: You come hither, my lord, to marry

speaker: FRIAR
text: this lady?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: No.

speaker: LEONATO
text: To be married to her.—Friar, you come to

speaker: LEONATO
text: marry her.

speaker: FRIAR
text: Lady, you come hither to be married to this

speaker: FRIAR
text: count?

speaker: HERO
text: I do.

speaker: FRIAR
text: If either of you know any inward impediment

speaker: FRIAR
text: why you should not be conjoined, I charge you on

speaker: FRIAR
text: your souls to utter it.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Know you any, Hero?

speaker: HERO
text: None, my lord.

speaker: FRIAR
text: Know you any, count?

speaker: LEONATO
text: I dare make his answer, none.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: O, what men dare do! What men may do!

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: What men daily do, not knowing what they do!

speaker: BENEDICK
text: How now, interjections? Why, then, some

speaker: BENEDICK
text: be of laughing, as ah, ha, he!

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Stand thee by, friar.—Father, by your leave,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Will you with free and unconstrainèd soul

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Give me this maid, your daughter?

speaker: LEONATO
text: As freely, son, as God did give her me.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: And what have I to give you back whose worth

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: May counterpoise this rich and precious gift?

speaker: PRINCE
text: Nothing, unless you render her again.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Sweet prince, you learn me noble thankfulness.—

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: There, Leonato, take her back again.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Give not this rotten orange to your friend.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: She’s but the sign and semblance of her honor.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Behold how like a maid she blushes here!

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: O, what authority and show of truth

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Can cunning sin cover itself withal!

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Comes not that blood as modest evidence

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: To witness simple virtue? Would you not swear,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: All you that see her, that she were a maid,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: By these exterior shows? But she is none.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: She knows the heat of a luxurious bed.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Her blush is guiltiness, not modesty.

speaker: LEONATO
text: What do you mean, my lord?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Not to be married,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Not to knit my soul to an approvèd wanton.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Dear my lord, if you in your own proof

speaker: LEONATO
text: Have vanquished the resistance of her youth,

speaker: LEONATO
text: And made defeat of her virginity—

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: I know what you would say: if I have known her,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: You will say she did embrace me as a husband,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: And so extenuate the forehand sin.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: No, Leonato,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: I never tempted her with word too large,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: But, as a brother to his sister, showed

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Bashful sincerity and comely love.

speaker: HERO
text: And seemed I ever otherwise to you?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Out on thee, seeming! I will write against it.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: You seem to me as Dian in her orb,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: As chaste as is the bud ere it be blown.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: But you are more intemperate in your blood

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Than Venus, or those pampered animals

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: That rage in savage sensuality.

speaker: HERO
text: Is my lord well that he doth speak so wide?

speaker: LEONATO
text: Sweet prince, why speak not you?

speaker: PRINCE
text: What should I speak?

speaker: PRINCE
text: I stand dishonored that have gone about

speaker: PRINCE
text: To link my dear friend to a common stale.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Are these things spoken, or do I but dream?

speaker:
DON
               
              JOHN
text: Sir, they are spoken, and these things are true.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: This looks not like a nuptial.

speaker: HERO
text: True! O God!

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Leonato, stand I here?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Is this the Prince? Is this the Prince’s brother?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Is this face Hero’s? Are our eyes our own?

speaker: LEONATO
text: All this is so, but what of this, my lord?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Let me but move one question to your daughter,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: And by that fatherly and kindly power

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: That you have in her, bid her answer truly.

speaker: LEONATO
text: I charge thee do so, as thou art my child.

speaker: HERO
text: O, God defend me, how am I beset!—

speaker: HERO
text: What kind of catechizing call you this?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: To make you answer truly to your name.

speaker: HERO
text: Is it not Hero? Who can blot that name

speaker: HERO
text: With any just reproach?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Marry, that can Hero!

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Hero itself can blot out Hero’s virtue.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: What man was he talked with you yesternight

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Out at your window betwixt twelve and one?

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Now, if you are a maid, answer to this.

speaker: HERO
text: I talked with no man at that hour, my lord.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Why, then, are you no maiden.—Leonato,

speaker: PRINCE
text: I am sorry you must hear. Upon mine honor,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Myself, my brother, and this grievèd count

speaker: PRINCE
text: Did see her, hear her, at that hour last night

speaker: PRINCE
text: Talk with a ruffian at her chamber window,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Who hath indeed, most like a liberal villain,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Confessed the vile encounters they have had

speaker: PRINCE
text: A thousand times in secret.

speaker:
DON
               
              JOHN
text: Fie, fie, they are not to be named, my lord,

speaker:
DON
               
              JOHN
text: Not to be spoke of!

speaker:
DON
               
              JOHN
text: There is not chastity enough in language,

speaker:
DON
               
              JOHN
text: Without offense, to utter them.—Thus, pretty lady,

speaker:
DON
               
              JOHN
text: I am sorry for thy much misgovernment.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: O Hero, what a Hero hadst thou been

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: If half thy outward graces had been placed

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: About thy thoughts and counsels of thy heart!

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: But fare thee well, most foul, most fair. Farewell,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: Thou pure impiety and impious purity.

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: For thee I’ll lock up all the gates of love

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: And on my eyelids shall conjecture hang,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: To turn all beauty into thoughts of harm,

speaker: CLAUDIO
text: And never shall it more be gracious.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Hath no man’s dagger here a point for me?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Why, how now, cousin, wherefore sink you down?

speaker:
DON
               
              JOHN
text: Come, let us go. These things, come thus to light,

speaker:
DON
               
              JOHN
text: Smother her spirits up.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: How doth the lady?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Dead, I think.—Help, uncle!—

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Hero, why Hero! Uncle! Signior Benedick! Friar!

speaker: LEONATO
text: O Fate, take not away thy heavy hand!

speaker: LEONATO
text: Death is the fairest cover for her shame

speaker: LEONATO
text: That may be wished for.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: How now, cousin Hero?

speaker: FRIAR
text: Have comfort, lady.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Dost thou look up?

speaker: FRIAR
text: Yea, wherefore should she not?

speaker: LEONATO
text: Wherefore? Why, doth not every earthly thing

speaker: LEONATO
text: Cry shame upon her? Could she here deny

speaker: LEONATO
text: The story that is printed in her blood?—

speaker: LEONATO
text: Do not live, Hero, do not ope thine eyes,

speaker: LEONATO
text: For, did I think thou wouldst not quickly die,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Thought I thy spirits were stronger than thy shames,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Myself would, on the rearward of reproaches,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Strike at thy life. Grieved I I had but one?

speaker: LEONATO
text: Chid I for that at frugal Nature’s frame?

speaker: LEONATO
text: O, one too much by thee! Why had I one?

speaker: LEONATO
text: Why ever wast thou lovely in my eyes?

speaker: LEONATO
text: Why had I not with charitable hand

speaker: LEONATO
text: Took up a beggar’s issue at my gates,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Who, smirchèd thus, and mired with infamy,

speaker: LEONATO
text: I might have said “No part of it is mine;

speaker: LEONATO
text: This shame derives itself from unknown loins”?

speaker: LEONATO
text: But mine, and mine I loved, and mine I praised,

speaker: LEONATO
text: And mine that I was proud on, mine so much

speaker: LEONATO
text: That I myself was to myself not mine,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Valuing of her—why she, O she, is fall’n

speaker: LEONATO
text: Into a pit of ink, that the wide sea

speaker: LEONATO
text: Hath drops too few to wash her clean again,

speaker: LEONATO
text: And salt too little which may season give

speaker: LEONATO
text: To her foul tainted flesh!

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Sir, sir, be patient.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: For my part, I am so attired in wonder

speaker: BENEDICK
text: I know not what to say.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: O, on my soul, my cousin is belied!

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Lady, were you her bedfellow last night?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: No, truly not, although until last night

speaker: BEATRICE
text: I have this twelvemonth been her bedfellow.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Confirmed, confirmed! O, that is stronger made

speaker: LEONATO
text: Which was before barred up with ribs of iron!

speaker: LEONATO
text: Would the two princes lie and Claudio lie,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Who loved her so that, speaking of her foulness,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Washed it with tears? Hence from her. Let her die!

speaker: FRIAR
text: Hear me a little,

speaker: FRIAR
text: For I have only silent been so long,

speaker: FRIAR
text: And given way unto this course of fortune,

speaker: FRIAR
text: By noting of the lady. I have marked

speaker: FRIAR
text: A thousand blushing apparitions

speaker: FRIAR
text: To start into her face, a thousand innocent shames

speaker: FRIAR
text: In angel whiteness beat away those blushes,

speaker: FRIAR
text: And in her eye there hath appeared a fire

speaker: FRIAR
text: To burn the errors that these princes hold

speaker: FRIAR
text: Against her maiden truth. Call me a fool,

speaker: FRIAR
text: Trust not my reading nor my observations,

speaker: FRIAR
text: Which with experimental seal doth warrant

speaker: FRIAR
text: The tenor of my book; trust not my age,

speaker: FRIAR
text: My reverence, calling, nor divinity,

speaker: FRIAR
text: If this sweet lady lie not guiltless here

speaker: FRIAR
text: Under some biting error.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Friar, it cannot be.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Thou seest that all the grace that she hath left

speaker: LEONATO
text: Is that she will not add to her damnation

speaker: LEONATO
text: A sin of perjury. She not denies it.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Why seek’st thou then to cover with excuse

speaker: LEONATO
text: That which appears in proper nakedness?

speaker: FRIAR
text: Lady, what man is he you are accused of?

speaker: HERO
text: They know that do accuse me. I know none.

speaker: HERO
text: If I know more of any man alive

speaker: HERO
text: Than that which maiden modesty doth warrant,

speaker: HERO
text: Let all my sins lack mercy!—O my father,

speaker: HERO
text: Prove you that any man with me conversed

speaker: HERO
text: At hours unmeet, or that I yesternight

speaker: HERO
text: Maintained the change of words with any creature,

speaker: HERO
text: Refuse me, hate me, torture me to death!

speaker: FRIAR
text: There is some strange misprision in the princes.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Two of them have the very bent of honor,

speaker: BENEDICK
text: And if their wisdoms be misled in this,

speaker: BENEDICK
text: The practice of it lives in John the Bastard,

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Whose spirits toil in frame of villainies.

speaker: LEONATO
text: I know not. If they speak but truth of her,

speaker: LEONATO
text: These hands shall tear her. If they wrong her honor,

speaker: LEONATO
text: The proudest of them shall well hear of it.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Time hath not yet so dried this blood of mine,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Nor age so eat up my invention,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Nor fortune made such havoc of my means,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Nor my bad life reft me so much of friends,

speaker: LEONATO
text: But they shall find, awaked in such a kind,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Both strength of limb and policy of mind,

speaker: LEONATO
text: Ability in means and choice of friends,

speaker: LEONATO
text: To quit me of them throughly.

speaker: FRIAR
text: Pause awhile,

speaker: FRIAR
text: And let my counsel sway you in this case.

speaker: FRIAR
text: Your daughter here the princes left for dead.

speaker: FRIAR
text: Let her awhile be secretly kept in,

speaker: FRIAR
text: And publish it that she is dead indeed.

speaker: FRIAR
text: Maintain a mourning ostentation,

speaker: FRIAR
text: And on your family’s old monument

speaker: FRIAR
text: Hang mournful epitaphs and do all rites

speaker: FRIAR
text: That appertain unto a burial.

speaker: LEONATO
text: What shall become of this? What will this do?

speaker: FRIAR
text: Marry, this well carried shall on her behalf

speaker: FRIAR
text: Change slander to remorse. That is some good.

speaker: FRIAR
text: But not for that dream I on this strange course,

speaker: FRIAR
text: But on this travail look for greater birth.

speaker: FRIAR
text: She, dying, as it must be so maintained,

speaker: FRIAR
text: Upon the instant that she was accused,

speaker: FRIAR
text: Shall be lamented, pitied, and excused

speaker: FRIAR
text: Of every hearer. For it so falls out

speaker: FRIAR
text: That what we have we prize not to the worth

speaker: FRIAR
text: Whiles we enjoy it, but being lacked and lost,

speaker: FRIAR
text: Why then we rack the value, then we find

speaker: FRIAR
text: The virtue that possession would not show us

speaker: FRIAR
text: Whiles it was ours. So will it fare with Claudio.

speaker: FRIAR
text: When he shall hear she died upon his words,

speaker: FRIAR
text: Th’ idea of her life shall sweetly creep

speaker: FRIAR
text: Into his study of imagination,

speaker: FRIAR
text: And every lovely organ of her life

speaker: FRIAR
text: Shall come appareled in more precious habit,

speaker: FRIAR
text: More moving, delicate, and full of life,

speaker: FRIAR
text: Into the eye and prospect of his soul,

speaker: FRIAR
text: Than when she lived indeed. Then shall he mourn,

speaker: FRIAR
text: If ever love had interest in his liver,

speaker: FRIAR
text: And wish he had not so accused her,

speaker: FRIAR
text: No, though he thought his accusation true.

speaker: FRIAR
text: Let this be so, and doubt not but success

speaker: FRIAR
text: Will fashion the event in better shape

speaker: FRIAR
text: Than I can lay it down in likelihood.

speaker: FRIAR
text: But if all aim but this be leveled false,

speaker: FRIAR
text: The supposition of the lady’s death

speaker: FRIAR
text: Will quench the wonder of her infamy.

speaker: FRIAR
text: And if it sort not well, you may conceal her,

speaker: FRIAR
text: As best befits her wounded reputation,

speaker: FRIAR
text: In some reclusive and religious life,

speaker: FRIAR
text: Out of all eyes, tongues, minds, and injuries.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Signior Leonato, let the Friar advise you.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: And though you know my inwardness and love

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Is very much unto the Prince and Claudio,

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Yet, by mine honor, I will deal in this

speaker: BENEDICK
text: As secretly and justly as your soul

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Should with your body.

speaker: LEONATO
text: Being that I flow in grief,

speaker: LEONATO
text: The smallest twine may lead me.

speaker: FRIAR
text: ’Tis well consented. Presently away,

speaker: FRIAR
text: For to strange sores strangely they strain the cure.—

speaker: FRIAR
text: Come, lady, die to live. This wedding day

speaker: FRIAR
text: Perhaps is but prolonged. Have patience and endure.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Lady Beatrice, have you wept all this while?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Yea, and I will weep a while longer.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: I will not desire that.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: You have no reason. I do it freely.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Surely I do believe your fair cousin is

speaker: BENEDICK
text: wronged.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Ah, how much might the man deserve of me

speaker: BEATRICE
text: that would right her!

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Is there any way to show such friendship?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: A very even way, but no such friend.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: May a man do it?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: It is a man’s office, but not yours.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: I do love nothing in the world so well as

speaker: BENEDICK
text: you. Is not that strange?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: As strange as the thing I know not. It were as

speaker: BEATRICE
text: possible for me to say I loved nothing so well as you,

speaker: BEATRICE
text: but believe me not, and yet I lie not; I confess

speaker: BEATRICE
text: nothing, nor I deny nothing. I am sorry for my

speaker: BEATRICE
text: cousin.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: By my sword, Beatrice, thou lovest me!

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Do not swear and eat it.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: I will swear by it that you love me, and I will

speaker: BENEDICK
text: make him eat it that says I love not you.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Will you not eat your word?

speaker: BENEDICK
text: With no sauce that can be devised to it. I

speaker: BENEDICK
text: protest I love thee.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Why then, God forgive me.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: What offense, sweet Beatrice?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: You have stayed me in a happy hour. I was

speaker: BEATRICE
text: about to protest I loved you.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: And do it with all thy heart.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: I love you with so much of my heart that

speaker: BEATRICE
text: none is left to protest.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Come, bid me do anything for thee.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Kill Claudio.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Ha! Not for the wide world.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: You kill me to deny it. Farewell.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Tarry, sweet Beatrice.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: I am gone, though I am here. There is no

speaker: BEATRICE
text: love in you. Nay, I pray you let me go.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Beatrice—

speaker: BEATRICE
text: In faith, I will go.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: We’ll be friends first.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: You dare easier be friends with me than

speaker: BEATRICE
text: fight with mine enemy.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Is Claudio thine enemy?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Is he not approved in the height a villain

speaker: BEATRICE
text: that hath slandered, scorned, dishonored my kinswoman?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: O, that I were a man! What, bear her in

speaker: BEATRICE
text: hand until they come to take hands, and then, with

speaker: BEATRICE
text: public accusation, uncovered slander, unmitigated

speaker: BEATRICE
text: rancor—O God, that I were a man! I would eat his

speaker: BEATRICE
text: heart in the marketplace.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Hear me, Beatrice—

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Talk with a man out at a window! A proper

speaker: BEATRICE
text: saying.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Nay, but Beatrice—

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Sweet Hero, she is wronged, she is slandered,

speaker: BEATRICE
text: she is undone.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Beat—

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Princes and counties! Surely a princely testimony,

speaker: BEATRICE
text: a goodly count, Count Comfect, a sweet

speaker: BEATRICE
text: gallant, surely! O, that I were a man for his sake! Or

speaker: BEATRICE
text: that I had any friend would be a man for my sake!

speaker: BEATRICE
text: But manhood is melted into curtsies, valor into

speaker: BEATRICE
text: compliment, and men are only turned into tongue,

speaker: BEATRICE
text: and trim ones, too. He is now as valiant as Hercules

speaker: BEATRICE
text: that only tells a lie and swears it. I cannot be a man

speaker: BEATRICE
text: with wishing; therefore I will die a woman with

speaker: BEATRICE
text: grieving.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Tarry, good Beatrice. By this hand, I love

speaker: BENEDICK
text: thee.

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Use it for my love some other way than

speaker: BEATRICE
text: swearing by it.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Think you in your soul the Count Claudio

speaker: BENEDICK
text: hath wronged Hero?

speaker: BEATRICE
text: Yea, as sure as I have a thought or a soul.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: Enough, I am engaged. I will challenge

speaker: BENEDICK
text: him. I will kiss your hand, and so I leave you. By

speaker: BENEDICK
text: this hand, Claudio shall render me a dear account.

speaker: BENEDICK
text: As you hear of me, so think of me. Go comfort your

speaker: BENEDICK
text: cousin. I must say she is dead, and so farewell.
